jokes.text: There's always one location in your neighbourhood, one store that's constantly changing hands... It's like some sort of Bermuda Triangle of retail.
jokes.setup: Universal experience of businesses failing in certain locations
jokes.punchline: Bermuda Triangle of retail - supernatural explanation for mundane phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor - takes mundane experience and finds perfect absurdist metaphor. Strong economy and originality.

jokes.text: Eventually, when aliens land... all these store owners will wander out in a daze, going, 'I thought there was gonna be more walk-in traffic.'
jokes.setup: Bermuda Triangle metaphor from previous joke
jokes.punchline: Mundane business concern delivered in alien abduction scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that maintains the metaphor while subverting expectations with banal business concern in extraordinary context.

jokes.text: Women don't want nice.
jokes.setup: Jerry mentions a woman thinks he's nice
jokes.punchline: George's cynical worldview delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect George character moment - neurotic, pessimistic wisdom delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: What kind of a sick society are we living in when nice is bad?
jokes.setup: His own statement that women don't want nice
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with moral outrage
jokes.explanation: Classic George self-contradiction - creates his own problem then rails against it. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: His family's probably back in Pakistan waiting for him to send back money. This is horrible.
jokes.punchline: George's tendency to catastrophize others' situations
jokes.explanation: Shows George's neurotic empathy - projects worst-case scenarios onto strangers.

jokes.text: I've never been a guinea pig. Been a sheep, been a toady...
jokes.setup: Describing his role in girlfriend's IQ test research
jokes.punchline: Lists other demeaning animal comparisons he's accepted
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing George's self-awareness about his submissive nature through clever animal metaphors.

jokes.text: I can't talk to you anymore.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's exasperated dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction to George's neurotic rambling.

jokes.text: I've been lying about my SAT scores for 15 years.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of long-term deception
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures George's pathetic dishonesty and insecurity. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What did I get, or what do I say I got?
jokes.setup: Jerry asks what he got on SATs
jokes.punchline: Clarifies between truth and lie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant follow-up that reveals the depth of George's deception in just a few words. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: Well, this I take to the grave.
jokes.setup: Building up to reveal his real SAT score
jokes.punchline: Treats mundane embarrassment as life-or-death secret
jokes.explanation: George's tendency to overdramatize minor embarrassments.

jokes.text: It's like a spider in the toilet, struggling for survival. And even though you know he's not gonna make it... you root for him for a second. Then you flush.
jokes.setup: Explaining his obsession with watching the failing restaurant
jokes.punchline: Dark metaphor ending with casual cruelty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark metaphor that perfectly captures morbid fascination. The casual 'then you flush' is perfectly cruel.

jokes.text: Well, it's a spider.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact justification for killing it
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that punctures George's elaborate metaphor with simple logic.

jokes.text: Bazooka Joe.
jokes.setup: Jerry chewing gum and blowing bubbles
jokes.punchline: Kramer's random observation about the gum
jokes.explanation: Minor moment - Kramer's random observation isn't particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: You gotta be on the lease to press the buzzer.
jokes.setup: Kramer about to buzz someone up
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous rule about buzzer privileges
jokes.explanation: Absurd Jerry rule-making about apartment etiquette.

jokes.text: Casus belli... It's Latin. I read it in some book. I just wanted to say it out loud.
jokes.punchline: Admits he has no real reason except showing off
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - pretentious yet pathetically honest about his pretension.

jokes.text: I could probably shoot him from here. I'd be doing us both a favour.
jokes.punchline: Dark joke about putting him out of misery
jokes.explanation: Continues the dark theme of 'mercy killing' the failing business owner.

jokes.text: Why is what I do so important? Why must I always be the focal point of attention? Let me just be. Let me live.
jokes.punchline: Melodramatic response to simple question
jokes.explanation: Classic George victimization complex - makes simple observation about him into persecution.

jokes.text: Why must you always be the focal point of attention? Why can't you just be? Why can't you live?
jokes.punchline: Jerry throws George's words back at him
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure - Jerry uses George's own words against him immediately.

jokes.text: Mine's 145.
jokes.setup: Discussion about not knowing IQ scores
jokes.punchline: Elaine immediately knows her exact high score
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Elaine's intelligence and slight vanity about it.

jokes.text: Get out of here. / You get out of here. / You get out of here.
jokes.punchline: Childish back-and-forth escalation
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke - functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hey, I love a good caper.
jokes.setup: Jerry and George planning IQ test cheating
jokes.punchline: Elaine's enthusiasm for criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Elaine's unexpected enthusiasm for schemes.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's what it is, isn't it? A caper.
jokes.punchline: George's pleased realization about the glamorous name
jokes.explanation: Shows George's delight in romanticizing his petty schemes.

jokes.text: How did you hear about us? / People. People are talking.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's obvious lie about non-existent buzz
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic joke about Jerry's polite deception.

jokes.text: A steaming hot, folded face cloth for your pleasure.
jokes.punchline: Overly formal description of simple wet towel
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Babu's overeager hospitality with flowery language.

jokes.text: Our specials are tacos... moussaka and franks and beans.
jokes.punchline: Completely random mix of cuisines
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of the restaurant's confused identity - the randomness is the joke.

jokes.text: Nobody thinks about people the way I do. Snap out of it, you stupid jerk. You're eating a turkey sandwich. You want a Nobel Prize?
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflates his own self-righteousness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-awareness moment - Jerry catches himself being self-congratulatory and deflates it perfectly.

jokes.text: This is the best tool we have today of measuring a person's intelligence.
jokes.setup: Jerry dismissing importance of IQ tests
jokes.punchline: George suddenly defending them when he needs to look smart
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George's self-serving arguments.

jokes.text: Eat something! Babu.
jokes.setup: Elaine ordering just tea and toast
jokes.punchline: Jerry's embarrassing attempt to boost Babu's sales
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment showing Jerry's misplaced helpfulness.

jokes.text: Statute. / What? / Statute of limitations. It's not a statue. / No, it's statue. / Fine. It's a sculpture of limitations.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic 'sculpture of limitations'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay escalation - 'sculpture of limitations' is a perfect absurdist punchline to the malapropism.

jokes.text: Would you please?! / Is it for a job or something? / Later! / You're positive it's statute? / Yes! Yes!
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to concentrate on test
jokes.punchline: Multiple people pestering him with questions
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation showing Jerry's mounting frustration with interruptions.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! It's all over the test! I'm out of time, anyway.
jokes.setup: Babu spilling food on the test
jokes.punchline: Test disaster at worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom physical disaster at the worst moment.

jokes.text: It's all right. She was cheating anyway.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's casual admission of the scheme
jokes.explanation: Great moment of casual moral relativism - Jerry dismisses the disaster because the whole thing was dishonest anyway.

jokes.text: I close down today. And when I open again, it will be a Pakistani restaurant. Thank you. Thank you so much. You're a very special person.
jokes.punchline: Babu's grateful acceptance and praise
jokes.explanation: Sets up the irony - Jerry's 'helpful' advice will likely make things worse.

jokes.text: I am such a great guy. Who else would've gone to the trouble to help this immigrant? I am special. My mother was right. Of course, I've never had Pakistani food. How bad could it be?
jokes.punchline: Admits he has no knowledge of what he recommended
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for future disaster - Jerry's ignorant confidence will backfire spectacularly.

jokes.text: I had a sandwich in my pocket.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd George logic - pocket sandwich is somehow plausible to him.

jokes.text: Why would I go out the door? The window's right here.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he climbed out window
jokes.punchline: Treats window as more logical exit
jokes.explanation: Perfect George logic - finds the most complicated solution and treats it as obvious. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: Eighty-five! 85 IQ.
jokes.punchline: Devastatingly low score delivered with emphasis
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the cheating scheme - the low score makes the elaborate plan seem even more pathetic.

jokes.text: Well, I'm no genius... but according to my calculations, he should be here in a few seconds.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's mock-intellectual analysis of obvious situation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to intelligence theme with self-deprecating irony.

jokes.text: Well, maybe the test was gender-biased. You know, a lot of questions on hunting and testicles.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous examples of gender bias
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd escalation - 'hunting and testicles' as test bias is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: It was the same look my father gave me when I told him I wanted to be a ventriloquist.
jokes.setup: Describing girlfriend's reaction to his low score
jokes.punchline: Random embarrassing childhood ambition
jokes.explanation: Perfect George backstory - ventriloquist ambition is perfectly pathetic and specific to his character.

jokes.text: The IQ tests always have that sample question at the beginning where they show you how to fill in the circle. This should be the first elimination point right there. Anybody goes outside that circle: 'You wanna come with us, please? Yeah, you're done.'
jokes.punchline: Treating basic instruction following as intelligence test
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational humor with perfect escalation to absurd consequences.

jokes.text: Babu... you know, I've gotta tell you, I never do this, but the shrimp... it's just a little stringy. You have any chicken?
jokes.setup: Jerry eating at the new Pakistani restaurant
jokes.punchline: Diplomatic criticism of his own recommendation
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Jerry's ignorant advice - his diplomatic complaint shows the food is terrible.

jokes.text: You make me change restaurant, but nobody come! You say make Pakistani. Babu Bhatt have only Pakistani restaurant. But where are people? You see people? Show me people! There are no people!
jokes.punchline: Babu's furious breakdown in broken English
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and payoff - Jerry's 'help' has destroyed Babu's business. The repetition builds beautifully.

jokes.text: You bad man! You very, very bad man.
jokes.punchline: Simple, devastating judgment in broken English
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff line - contrasts with his earlier 'very kind man' praise. Became iconic quote.

jokes.text: Bad man? Could my mother have been wrong?
jokes.punchline: Jerry's mock self-doubt about maternal validation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to his earlier self-congratulation - mock-serious questioning of fundamental beliefs.

jokes.text: One fifty-one.
jokes.setup: Jerry asks about second IQ test results
jokes.punchline: Impossibly high score after previous 85
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd reversal - from 85 to 151 shows the meaninglessness of the whole enterprise.

jokes.text: It's tough to do a good deed. Let's look at your professional good-deed doers: Your Lone Rangers, your Supermen... your Batmen, your Spider-Men, your Elastic Men. They're all wearing disguises. Masks over their faces, secret identities. Don't want people to know who they are. Too much aggravation.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on his failed attempt to help Babu
jokes.punchline: Superhero analysis leading to practical explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational premise with perfect escalation. Takes mundane experience and finds universal truth through superhero logic.

jokes.text: 'Superman, thanks for saving my life... but did you have to come through my wall? I'm renting here. They got a security deposit. What am I supposed to do?'
jokes.punchline: Mundane practical concerns after heroic rescue
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and closer - the security deposit concern is brilliantly mundane after superhero rescue. Classic Seinfeld mundane-meets-extraordinary.
